explanations.5: The double equals (==) checks if the firstname is an empty string; if nobody passes a name to the function, this condition will be true.
explanations.8: The dot (.) is PHP's way of joining strings together, so this creates 'Hello ' combined with whatever name was passed in.
explanations.14: This calls sayHello() without passing any argument, so $firstname will be empty and the function will return 'Hello World!'.
